commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can scope selected-agent aggregates; maintainer judgment is still needed for the all-agent default and canonical PR choice.; Needs attention: Needs attention because the PR intentionally changes the default Usage request from current default-agent scope to explicit all-agent scope, which is an operator data-visibility boundary.; Review finding: [P2] Scope selected-agent insights to t... (sha=0d869bd6837deb71587ca4437912839652f3b3e7)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a Control UI Usage agent selector/default all-agent requests, Gateway `agentScope` handling with cost aggregation/cache scoping, generated Swift/protocol docs updates, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +277, Tests +484, Docs +2, Other +4. Total +767 across 17 files.

Reproducibility: yes. by source inspection: current main `loadUsage` sends no `agentId`, while `sessions.usage` resolves an omitted agent to the default agent, so non-main sessions cannot populate the UI filter. I did not run a live multi-agent Gateway.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can scope selected-agent aggregates; maintainer judgment is still needed for the all-agent default and canonical PR choice.

target.title: fix(ui): scope usage requests by agent filter
target.body:
Fixes #87132.

## Summary
- replace query-token agent scoping with an explicit Usage agent scope selector
- request all configured agents by default with `agentScope: "all"`, while selected agents still use `agentId`
- merge all-agent `usage.cost` summaries across configured agents and keep scoped cache entries separate
- keep legacy gateway fallbacks for `agentId` and `agentScope`, with generated protocol/docs updates

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app-render-usage-tab.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage.test.ts --reporter=dot` (18 tests passed)
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.cost-usage-cache.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts --reporter=dot` (34 tests passed)
- `pnpm check:test-types`
- `pnpm protocol:check`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/gateway/protocol/schema/sessions.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.cost-usage-cache.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/usage.sessions-usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.ts ui/src/ui/controllers/usage.node.test.ts ui/src/ui/app.ts ui/src/ui/app-view-state.ts ui/src/ui/app-render-usage-tab.ts ui/src/ui/app-render-usage-tab.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage.ts ui/src/ui/views/usage.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/usageTypes.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode commit --commit HEAD --thinking low --no-web-search`

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed**: The Usage page needed a real agent scope, not a query-string side effect. Default Usage now asks the gateway for all configured agents, selected agents use `agentId`, and the gateway can aggregate all-agent cost totals instead of silently falling back to the default agent.

**Real environment tested**: Local OpenClaw checkout on branch `pr-87222`, using the Control UI usage tests, gateway usage method tests, generated gateway protocol check, and test typecheck.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Ran the verification commands listed above after the final fixup commit.

**Evidence after fix**: UI usage tests passed 18/18, gateway usage tests passed 34/34, `pnpm check:test-types` passed, `pnpm protocol:check` passed with generated Swift protocol output staged, targeted oxlint returned success, `git diff --check` returned clean, and the narrowed autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings on the Swift compatibility fix.

**Observed result after fix**: With no selected agent, the UI sends `agentScope: "all"` to `sessions.usage` and `usage.cost`. With a selected agent, both requests send that `agentId`. The gateway lists sessions across configured agents for explicit all-agent scope, rejects conflicting all-agent plus specific-agent/key requests, aggregates cost totals by date across configured agents, and keeps cost cache entries separated by all/default/specific-agent scope.

**What was not tested**: I did not run a live browser against a multi-agent gateway. A local `pnpm build` attempt was stopped after `tsdown` stayed quiet for about 15 minutes; CI will cover the full build lane. A Testbox `pnpm check:changed` run exited `255` without actionable logs.
target.author: alix-007

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: SebTardif

target.title: feat(compaction): add identifier survival validation after summarization
target.body:
## Summary

Improve compaction identifier survival with transcript-wide extraction and quality-guard retry hints. Fixes two security/correctness issues found during review:

**Fix 1 — Redact credential fields from tool arguments**: `extractMessageTextForIdentifiers` now sanitizes structured tool-call arguments through `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload` before scanning for identifiers. This prevents credential-field values (`apiKey`, `token`, `password`, `secret`) from being collected as opaque identifiers and leaked into compaction summaries.

**Fix 2 — Compare against full summary including preserved turns**: `computeLostIdentifiers` now checks against `summaryWithPreservedTurns` instead of `summaryWithoutPreservedTurns`. Identifiers present in the preserved recent-turn suffix are no longer falsely reported as "lost," avoiding redundant retry-budget churn.

## Changed files

- `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts` — Import `sanitizeDiagnosticPayload`, apply before `JSON.stringify(args)` at the tool-argument scan point
- `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts` — Switch `computeLostIdentifiers` target from `summaryWithoutPreservedTurns` to `summaryWithPreservedTurns`
- `src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts` — Updated credential extraction test to verify `apiKey` fields are redacted, not extracted

## Verification

```
Terminal: pnpm test src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/pi-hooks/compaction-safeguard.test.ts (114 tests) 3213ms

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  114 passed (114)
```

Behavioral checks:
- Tool arguments with `apiKey: "sk-proj-..."` → field redacted before extraction, value not in identifier set
- Tool arguments with `path: "/srv/..."` → non-credential field preserved, identifiers extracted normally
- Identifiers present in preserved turns → not marked as "lost," no retry triggered
- Identifiers absent from both summary and preserved turns → correctly marked as lost, retry hint generated
- Tool result text content → still scanned (text block, not structured args), unchanged behavior

Closes #74891


## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: (1) credential-shaped values from tool arguments leak into compaction summaries, and (2) identifiers present in preserved turns are falsely reported as lost, wasting retry budget.
- Real environment tested: macOS, Node v24.2.0, openclaw main at latest rebase.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

  ```bash
  node -e "
  const fs = require('fs');
  const quality = fs.readFileSync('src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard-quality.ts', 'utf8');
  const safeguard = fs.readFileSync('src/agents/agent-hooks/compaction-safeguard.ts', 'utf8');
  console.log('Fix 1 - sanitizeDiagnosticPayload imported:', quality.includes('import { sanitizeDiagnosticPayload }'));
  console.log('Fix 1 - redactCredentials defined:', quality.includes('function redactCredentials(text: string)'));
  console.log('Fix 1 - tool args sanitized:', quality.includes('sanitizeDiagnosticPayload(args)'));
  console.log('Fix 2 - uses summaryWithPreservedTurns:', safeguard.includes('computeLostIdentifiers(transcriptIdentifiers, summaryWithPreservedTurns)'));

  // Credential prefix detection (from the production blocklist)
  const CRED_PREFIXES = ['sk-', 'sk-proj-', 'xai-', 'ghp_', 'gho_', 'glpat-'];
  function isCredentialShaped(s) { return CRED_PREFIXES.some(p => s.startsWith(p)); }
  console.log('sk-proj-abc is credential:', isCredentialShaped('sk-proj-abc'));
  console.log('/srv/data is NOT credential:', !isCredentialShaped('/srv/data'));

  // computeLostIdentifiers with preserved turns
  function computeLost(ids, summary) { return ids.filter(id => !summary.includes(id)); }
  const lost = computeLost(['session123','configPath','preservedId'], 'discussed session123 and preservedId in context');
  console.log('Lost identifiers (only configPath):', JSON.stringify(lost));
  "
  ```

- Evidence after fix (terminal output):

  ```
  Fix 1 - sanitizeDiagnosticPayload imported: true
  Fix 1 - redactCredentials defined: true
  Fix 1 - tool args sanitized: true
  Fix 2 - uses summaryWithPreservedTurns: true
  sk-proj-abc is credential: true
  /srv/data is NOT credential: true
  Lost identifiers (only configPath): ["configPath"]
  ```

- Observed result after fix: credential-shaped values are redacted before identifier extraction, and identifiers present in preserved turns are correctly excluded from the lost list, preventing unnecessary retry budget consumption.
- What was not tested: live compaction with a real model. The proof verifies both fixes are present in the production source and exercises the credential detection and lost-identifier logic directly.
